title: lagerstroemia undulata
intro: Lagerstroemia undulata, also known as the Indian crape myrtle or tree mallow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lagerstroemia undulata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
